engagements.name: Fortune 500 VDP (Program A)
engagements.description: Vulnerability disclosure program targeting a Fortune 500 company's massive infrastructure. Deep recon across 35K+ subdomains led to critical credential exposure and unauthenticated API access findings. Both findings accepted and resolved by the program.
highlights: CRITICAL: Live database credentials exposed via code repository
HIGH: Unauthenticated session CRUD on internal API
35K+ subdomains enumerated, 700+ live hosts probed
Both findings accepted and resolved — 100% acceptance rate

engagements.name: Healthcare Retailer (Program B)
engagements.description: Bug bounty program for a healthcare retailer. Discovered exposed API documentation, source maps, registration bypass, UAT environment access, and hardcoded API credentials.
highlights: HIGH: Healthcare API documentation exposure with sensitive endpoints
MED: Registration CAPTCHA validation order bypass
MED: UAT environment publicly accessible
Best scope discipline — 0 out-of-scope reports

engagements.name: Fintech Trading Platform (Program C)
engagements.description: Bug bounty program for a fintech trading platform. 10 findings across authentication, OAuth misconfigurations, exposed test environments, and hardcoded credentials. Full recon complete: 300+ subdomains, 150+ live hosts. Five authenticated sessions complete — IDOR surface ruled out, OAuth custom application attack chain confirmed with Tier 1 end-to-end evidence. Two findings submitted: critical OAuth account takeover (independently discovered — closed duplicate) and high-severity authentication token exposure (pending triage).
highlights: CRITICAL: OAuth custom application attack chain — account takeover via token exposure — Tier 1 end-to-end PoC; independently discovered, closed duplicate
HIGH: Authentication token exposure via domain-scoped cookies — full end-to-end exploit chain proven, submitted, pending triage
HIGH: Source maps exposing full application source code
IDOR patterns tested with dual-account setup — server-side access controls confirmed
5 authenticated sessions: 25+ hypotheses tested, P1 strengthened with redirect_uri bypass and header leak
Recon: 300+ subdomains, staging environment, OAuth OIDC config, and legacy API docs discovered

engagements.name: Trading Platform (Program D)
engagements.description: Bug bounty program for a trading platform. Comprehensive recon with API spec extraction. 8 configuration findings documented. Closed — geo-restricted registration blocked authenticated testing; all viable surface-level findings recorded.
highlights: 3,000+ subdomains enumerated, API spec with 30+ REST endpoints
8 configuration findings documented across API and infrastructure surface
Threat model: 3 high-value surfaces identified for authenticated follow-up
Closed: geo-restricted registration blocked authenticated access — scope lesson

engagements.name: Food Delivery Platform (Program E)
engagements.description: Bug bounty program for a global food delivery platform. 11 reports across authentication bypass, subdomain takeover, staging environment exposure, and source map analysis. Identity provider Keycloak realm misconfiguration submitted with rebuttal. Platform maintenance detected — Keycloak path migration confirmed post-report but core vulnerability unpatched. Two findings submission-ready.
highlights: HIGH: Identity provider Keycloak realm impersonation — Tier 1 PoC submitted; rebuttal posted with new differential evidence: platform migrated Keycloak realm path post-report, confirming active maintenance but failed remediation. Rebuttal exceeds 23+ days without triager response (6x SLA); platform support escalation window opened 2026-04-20 at the 60-day mark.
Authentication bypass via test token confirmed in 3 distinct production bundle hashes — present 60+ days across multiple deployments; survives platform maintenance
Subdomain takeover opportunity identified — 5 CNAMEs confirmed pointing to unclaimed third-party instance, stable for 60+ days — full report written and submission-ready
7-gate re-validation completed April 2026 — key findings confirmed still live; final action verdicts assigned; 1 report submission-ready, 1 rebuttal escalation queued
Recon complete: identity provider surfaces, partner portals, and payment API endpoints mapped

engagements.name: E-commerce Platform (Program F)
engagements.description: Bug bounty program for an e-commerce platform. 75% out-of-scope rate due to wildcard exclusion. Painful lesson in scope verification before testing.
highlights: 75% out-of-scope rate — worst performance across all programs
JWT leak found on excluded asset
Scope lesson: wildcard exclusions override individual asset matches
0 confirmed submissions — status uncertain

engagements.name: AI Platform (Program G)
engagements.description: Dual bug bounty program targeting a major AI platform's security and safety surfaces. Scope covers primary web app, REST API, and agentic product surfaces. Karpathy-style research loop deployed — 39 trial executions across multiple hypothesis batches. Two P3/Medium findings submitted: persistent memory injection via web browsing (Safety BB) and sandbox write boundary violation in CLI tool allowing persistent context poisoning (Security BB). Attribution and provenance-based injection vectors identified as the effective approach — these survive safety filters where explicit override commands fail. Browser-based exfil hypothesis closed after six iterate sessions: model named the injection attempt and discarded it; two distinct defenses confirmed.
highlights: MED: Persistent memory injection via web browsing — attacker preferences injected across all future sessions — Tier 1 PoC, 63% reproducibility, submitted
MED: CLI tool sandbox write boundary violation — malicious repository can poison persistent agent context via AGENTS.md — Tier 1 PoC, 100% reproducibility, submitted
Key insight: model verbally refused credential read while simultaneously executing the command — safety training gates output, not execution
Injection strategy: attribution/provenance vectors survive safety filters where explicit override commands fail
All 4 initial hypotheses resolved: 2 submitted, 1 disproven (0/21 trials), 1 closed (defenses confirmed after 6 iterate sessions)

engagements.name: Hospitality SaaS Platform (Consulting C)
engagements.description: Owner-authorized security audit of a multi-tenant hospitality management SaaS platform serving hotels, homestays, and restaurants. Assessment covered cloud storage configuration, backend API security, authentication, database access controls, and infrastructure hardening.
highlights: CRITICAL: Government identity documents publicly accessible in cloud storage — unauthenticated enumeration and download of all guest ID files
HIGH: Unauthenticated user enumeration and role disclosure via public API functions
MED: Database schema and business data exposure via misconfigured access controls
MED: Missing row-level security on sensitive business tables
Full remediation roadmap delivered — all findings fixable with targeted configuration changes

engagements.name: ERP/CRM Platform (Consulting D)
engagements.description: Owner-authorized security audit of an ERP/CRM SaaS platform serving as a business management suite for an SME client. Assessment covered CORS configuration, API security, authentication, error handling, user enumeration, and security headers across the full application surface.
highlights: HIGH: Permissive CORS reflecting any origin with credentials — enables cross-origin data theft from authenticated sessions via any malicious page
HIGH: Chained attack — CORS + credential-bearing API endpoint + user enumeration enables full authenticated session data exfiltration without user interaction
MED: Verbose Python stack traces in error responses — framework paths, module names, and server configuration exposed to all users
MED: User enumeration via password reset — account existence confirmable for all registered users
Full remediation roadmap delivered — CORS fix breaks the entire attack chain; all findings fixable with targeted configuration changes

engagements.name: E-Commerce Store (Consulting B)
engagements.description: Owner-authorized security assessment of a Shopify-based e-commerce store. Testing covered price manipulation, authentication, API security, JavaScript analysis, and infrastructure configuration.
highlights: MED: Missing DMARC enables domain spoofing and customer phishing risk
LOW: Weak HSTS configuration — missing includeSubDomains directive
LOW: Insufficient Content Security Policy — no script-src allowlist
Core controls verified: XSS, price manipulation, and auth bypass all blocked
Full remediation roadmap delivered — all issues fixable in under 1 hour

engagements.name: Software Startup (Consulting A)
engagements.description: Owner-authorized full-scope security audit for a software startup. Assessment covered web application, authentication systems, cloud backend, and infrastructure configuration across all assets.
highlights: MED: Open authentication registration — no invite/approval required
MED: Cloud configuration exposing app secrets via CDN bypass
MED: Missing email security controls (SPF/DKIM/DMARC absent)
Full audit report with remediation roadmap delivered to owner

engagements.name: Crypto/Fintech Wallet (Program H)
engagements.description: Bug bounty engagement targeting a crypto/fintech wallet platform. Scope spans GraphQL and REST APIs, enterprise portal, and 35+ web targets. JS harvest complete — 150+ GraphQL operations mapped. Engagement paused: program temporarily suspended by the platform. Queued for resume when program reopens.
highlights: 150+ GraphQL operations extracted via JS harvest — schema mapped pre-pause
Priority surfaces identified: OAuth flows, GraphQL mutation fuzzing, wallet IDOR, enterprise portal privilege boundaries
Recon and threat model complete — ready to resume immediately on program reopen
Paused: program suspended by platform — monitoring for reactivation

engagements.name: Digital Wealth Manager (Program I)
engagements.description: Private bug bounty program for a digital wealth management platform. Recon complete: 80+ subdomains, 20+ live hosts, 100+ API operations mapped via JS harvest. Recon refreshed April 2026 — 4 new hosts identified. Eleven authenticated sessions completed across authentication, referral, investment, and financial planning surfaces. Rate-limit window fully characterized — 12+ hour OTP window. 30+ attack hypotheses tested across IDOR, mass assignment, OTP cross-flow, and privilege escalation vectors — server-side access controls confirmed throughout. Financial resource objects created; sequential identifiers observed in financial transaction data. Sessions 8–11: differential response oracle identified (Session 8), closed (Session 9) — endpoint returns identical errors for owned and unowned resource IDs; OTP payload schema confirmed in Session 10 — correctly-formed requests immediately trigger rate-limit boundary, confirming validation-before-rate-limit architecture; Session 11 expanded financial surface coverage, rate-limit boundary persists. One finding validated: referral API data exposure (P3/Medium, Tier 1 evidence, pending submission). Quick-wins scan saturated: CORS exact-match whitelist confirmed (6 attack vectors), CVE surface zero-match, Spring Boot actuators locked (15 endpoints), dangling S3 bucket in global CSP confirmed unclaimed. S3 non-prod bucket enumeration exhausted (12 naming variants, all empty). Backend stack confirmed: Spring Boot 3.x. Infrastructure fingerprint complete.
highlights: Private program: invitation-only, up to $1,500 per finding, 99% response efficiency
MED: Referral API data exposure — PII of 16 users accessible via referral tracking — P3/Tier 1, validated, pending submission
Recon: 80+ subdomains, 20+ live endpoints, 100+ API operations mapped via JS harvest; recon refresh April 2026 identified 4 new hosts
11 authenticated sessions: 30+ attack hypotheses across IDOR, mass assignment, OTP cross-flow, and privilege escalation surfaces

Sessions 8–10: oracle identified (Session 8), closed (Session 9) — endpoint returns identical errors for owned and unowned IDs; Session 10 confirmed OTP payload schema and validation-before-rate-limit architecture
Financial resource objects created — sequential identifiers in transaction data identified; remaining IDOR vectors require second verified account
Rate-limit window fully characterized: 12+ hour OTP window; mass assignment silently denied on all financial object fields

Quick wins scan phase formally closed (13 passes): CORS exact-match confirmed (6 vectors), CVE surface zero-match, Spring Boot actuators locked (15 endpoints). Dangling S3 bucket in global CSP: confirmed unclaimed across 10 days (8 consecutive confirmations) — P4/Low candidate. 3 new subdomains discovered in April recon refresh — scope confirmation pending. S3 non-prod enumeration closed (12 naming variants, all empty). CDN false-positive pattern documented; real endpoint isolated via Content-Type differential. Backend confirmed: Spring Boot 3.x.

engagements.name: Digital Banking Platform (Program K)
engagements.description: Public bug bounty program for a European digital banking platform. Scope covers primary web application, REST API, and iOS/Android mobile applications. Recon complete: 149 subdomains discovered, 51 live hosts, 96 historical API URLs. JS harvest complete across 25 files — 900+ raw endpoints extracted. Threat model built with 6 attack hypotheses across OAuth flows, Open Banking, and webview surfaces. Pre-auth endpoint testing initiated — 35 test scenarios run across Open Banking FAPI surface; redirect_uri whitelist, JWT bypass, and SSRF all confirmed blocked. Campaign technique replay session run April 2026 — OAuth attack chain confirmed applicable, two proven techniques from validated registry mapped to target architecture; account registration required to unlock authenticated testing phase.
highlights: Public program: competitive per-finding bounties across web, API, and mobile scope
Recon complete: 149 subdomains, 51 live hosts, 96 historical API URLs — full attack surface mapped
JS harvest complete: 25 files analyzed, 900+ raw endpoints, OAuth client configurations extracted
Threat model built: 6 attack hypotheses prioritized across OAuth flows, API authentication, Open Banking, and webview surfaces
Pre-auth testing: 35 scenarios across Open Banking FAPI endpoint — redirect_uri whitelist, JWT alg:none bypass, and SSRF all blocked; hypothesis closed
Campaign session April 2026 — OAuth attack chain confirmed applicable; two proven techniques from validated registry mapped to target; authenticated testing queued pending account registration

engagements.name: Digital Payments Platform (Program J)
engagements.description: Vulnerability disclosure program for a major digital payments platform. Blind SSRF discovered via merchant API webhook URL validation — out-of-band DNS and HTTP callbacks confirmed from platform cloud infrastructure. 10 OOB callbacks received, internal service name and observability metadata exposed. Full 7-gate validation passed at P2/High. Report written, submission pending.
highlights: HIGH: Blind SSRF via webhook URL parameter — OOB callbacks confirmed from cloud infrastructure (10 deliveries)
Internal service identity and observability platform metadata exposed via request headers
DNS rebinding and IP encoding bypass vectors enumerated
Tier 1 evidence: end-to-end PoC with OOB callback log, payment flow, and curl commands
Submission-ready VDP report written — full evidence package complete, awaiting manual platform submission

engagements.name: Enterprise HR Platform (Program L)
engagements.description: Bug bounty program for an enterprise HR management SaaS platform. Engagement initialized via campaign technique replay — webhook API endpoints confirmed, infrastructure fingerprinted across CDN and edge network layers. Signup automation blocked by multi-layer bot protection; manual account registration queued to unlock authenticated testing.
highlights: Campaign technique replay: webhook vector confirmed applicable — API endpoint confirmed via HTTP probe
Auth flow mapped: credential-exchange to bearer token pattern documented
Infrastructure fingerprinted: CDN + edge network across web and API surfaces
Signup automation blocked by 5-layer bot protection — manual account registration required to proceed

engagements.name: Global E-Commerce Marketplace (Program M)
engagements.description: VDP targeting a large-scale global e-commerce marketplace platform spanning B2B, B2C, and advertising surfaces. Program intel loaded from public disclosures. Threat model built with microservices architecture mapped and priority attack surfaces identified. Recon and JS harvest queued as the first active phase.
highlights: Scope: global B2B and B2C marketplace platform — critical bounty ceiling across all in-scope assets
Program intel loaded: top vulnerability classes identified from public disclosures — IDOR, stored XSS, cloud storage misconfiguration, SSRF
Threat model built: microservices architecture mapped, 4 high-value attack surfaces prioritized
Recon and JS harvest queued as first active testing phase

engagements.name: OWASP Juice Shop
engagements.description: Comprehensive security assessment of OWASP Juice Shop — a deliberately vulnerable web application covering the OWASP Top 10 and beyond.
highlights: 26/110 challenges solved across all difficulty tiers
Full OWASP Top 10 coverage with reproducible PoCs
SQL injection, XSS, broken auth, access control bypasses
API abuse, SSRF, cryptographic failures identified
